basic_details.short_information: Nationality: Candidates must be Indian citizens as per the general eligibility conditions of Indian government recruitment.Residence: Many OSSC/OSSSC and district-level posts require an Odisha resident/nativity certificate, and passing Odia at the Middle English (Class 7) standard is required for several state posts; this requirement varies by notification.Educational Qualification: Ranges from 8th/10th pass for Group D posts to postgraduate and professional degrees for gazetted posts, as listed in the "Jobs by Qualification" section above.Age: See the Age Limit section below.

application_fee.details: The application fee varies by recruiting body and post, and SC, ST and PwD candidates from Odisha are usually charged a reduced fee or exempted, subject to the rules in the specific notification. The exact fee and accepted payment mode for any post are Not specified here; refer to that post's official notification.

age_limit.details: The minimum age for most Odisha government posts is 18 years, and the general upper age limit ranges from 32 to 38 years for state posts, with age relaxation for SEBC, SC, ST, women and PwD candidates as per Odisha Government rules. The exact age limit and relaxation for a specific post is Not specified here and must be confirmed from that post's official notification, as it varies by recruiting body and post.

qualification.details: Odisha government jobs are available across every education level. salary_details.details: Salaries for Odisha government jobs follow the state pay matrix. Group D posts start at the lower pay levels, while a Junior Clerk is placed in Level-4 with a basic pay of around ₹19,900 plus DA, HRA and other allowances; Group A officers draw considerably higher scales. The exact pay level and allowances for any post are specified in that post's official recruitment notification.

selection_process: Most Odisha government jobs involve a written examination
followed by a skill or physical test where applicable
and then document verification. OPSC posts may also include an interview stage
OSSSC and Odisha Police posts rely mainly on written and physical stages (PET/PST/PMT for uniformed posts). The exact selection stages vary by post and are described in the official notification.

syllabus.details: The syllabus for OSSSC Nursing Officer recruitment is published along with the official notification on osssc.gov.in and is also linked from the FreeJobAlert Syllabus section.

how_to_apply: Open the official portal of the recruiting body (OPSC, OSSC, OSSSC or Odisha Police).
Register with a valid email ID and mobile number to create your login.
Fill the form with personal, educational and category details and upload your photo, signature and certificates.
Pay the application fee online and submit before the last date.
Save and print the confirmation page for future reference.

content_sections.heading: Latest Odisha Government Jobs 2026 - Apply Online for Odisha Govt Recruitment
content_sections.text: This page tracks every Odisha Government Job notification released by OPSC, OSSC, OSSSC, Odisha Police, District Courts, NHM Odisha, RRB Bhubaneswar and central government offices located in Odisha. It helps 10th pass, 12th pass, ITI, Diploma, Graduate and Post Graduate candidates find vacancy details, eligibility, last dates and direct apply-online links in one place. This page is updated daily as new notifications are released. Along with fresh recruitment notifications, this page links to upcoming Odisha recruitments, admit cards, exam results, answer keys and syllabus for Odisha government exams. Bookmark this page to stay updated on OAS, OSSC CGL, OSSSC combined recruitment, Odisha Police, Anganwadi and district court vacancies without missing an application deadline.

content_sections.heading: Quick Summary - Odisha Government Jobs 2026
content_sections.text: This page consolidates verified Odisha recruitment notifications from official sources instead of making you search multiple department websites separately. Every entry links directly to the source notification and, where available, the apply-online portal. Vacancy figures, qualifications and last dates shown below are taken directly from the respective official notifications; where a detail is not mentioned in the source notification, it is marked "Not specified" rather than estimated.

content_sections.heading: Government Departments Recruiting in Odisha
content_sections.text: Odisha Public Service Commission (OPSC) Odisha Staff Selection Commission (OSSC) Odisha Sub-ordinate Staff Selection Commission (OSSSC) Odisha Police Recruitment Board (OPRB) / Odisha Police Odisha High Court and District Courts (e.g. Balasore, Deogarh, Kendrapara, Jajpur, Ganjam, Balangir, Keonjhar District Courts) Women & Child Development (WCD) Odisha National Health Mission (NHM) Odisha / District Health Societies AIIMS Bhubaneswar Revenue & Disaster Management Department Forest & Environment Department School & Mass Education Department / Samagra Shiksha RRB Bhubaneswar / East Coast Railway (ECoR) Odisha Power Sector Undertakings - OPTCL, GRIDCO, OHPC PSUs based in Odisha - NALCO, MCL, IOCL Paradip, OMC, SAIL Central Universities and Institutes - NIT Rourkela, NISER Bhubaneswar, Central University of Odisha, Berhampur University Odisha Gramya Bank, Utkal Grameen Bank, Odisha State Co-operative Bank

content_sections.heading: Odisha Govt Jobs by Qualification
content_sections.text: Odisha government jobs are available across every education level. FreeJobAlert.com currently lists 13,191 government job openings nationwide for 10th pass candidates, 17,626 for 12th pass, 18,540 for Diploma holders, 21,851 for ITI candidates, 11,870 for B.Tech/B.E graduates, 9,364 for B.Com graduates, 21,458 for any-graduate eligibility and 1,115 for post-graduates; these figures reflect current listings across the FreeJobAlert network and are not exclusive to Odisha. 10th Pass - Group D, Peon/Attendant, Forest Guard, Home Guard, Warder, IRB Constable 12th Pass - Junior Clerk, Amin, Data Entry Operator, Police Constable, Excise Constable, Fireman, Forester ITI/Diploma - Junior Engineer (Civil/Mechanical/Electrical), Lineman, Surveyor, Technical Trade posts Graduate (Any Degree) - OSSC CGL posts, Revenue Inspector, Sub-Inspector of Police, Auditor, Junior Teacher, OAS (through OPSC) Nursing/Pharmacy/Paramedical - Nursing Officer (GNM/B.Sc Nursing), Pharmacist (D.Pharm), Lab Technician (DMLT) Post Graduate - Post Graduate Teacher (PGT), Lecturer, Assistant Professor Professional Degree (B.Tech/MBBS/BDS) - Assistant Engineer, Assistant Surgeon/Medical Officer, Specialist posts

content_sections.heading: Odisha Govt Jobs by Department
content_sections.text: NHM Odisha, AIIMS Bhubaneswar and district health societies regularly recruit Nursing Officers, Pharmacists, GDMOs and Medicine Specialists, often through walk-in interviews at the district level. Teaching recruitment runs through the School & Mass Education Department, OAVS and OPSC. Most posts require the Odisha Teacher Eligibility Test (OTET) or CTET along with the teaching qualification: Junior Teacher/Sikshya Sahayak (Primary) needs 10+2 with CT/D.El.Ed and OTET Paper-I; Upper Primary needs Graduation with B.Ed/CT and OTET Paper-II; TGT needs a relevant Bachelor's degree with B.Ed and TET; OAVS PGT needs Post Graduation with B.Ed; and Lecturer/Assistant Professor posts through OPSC need Post Graduation with NET/SLET. Odisha Police, Excise, Fire Service and Prisons recruit through OPRB with a written exam plus physical measurement/efficiency test. Police Constable and Excise Constable need 12th pass; Sub-Inspector, Sergeant and Excise Sub-Inspector need Graduation; Fireman needs 12th pass with physical efficiency; Warder and Home Guard need 10th pass. Odisha High Court and District Courts (Balasore, Deogarh, Kendrapara, Jajpur, Ganjam, Balangir, Keonjhar and others) regularly recruit Junior Clerk-cum-Copyist, Stenographer and Junior Typist posts, mostly through offline application. Assistant Engineer (B.E/B.Tech) posts are recruited through OPSC; Junior Engineer (Diploma) through OSSC and department notifications; OPTCL/GRIDCO/OHPC, NALCO, MCL and OMC run their own PSU-level engineering recruitment. RRB Bhubaneswar and RRC East Coast Railway recruit for NTPC categories (12th/Graduate), Group D/Level 1 (10th/ITI), Assistant Loco Pilot and Technician (10th with ITI/Diploma), Junior Engineer (Diploma/Degree) and RPF Constable/Sub-Inspector. Odisha Gramya Bank, Utkal Grameen Bank and the Odisha State Co-operative Bank recruit through IBPS RRB, alongside nationalised bank vacancies (IBPS PO/Clerk, SBI PO/Clerk, RBI Assistant/Grade B) open to candidates from Odisha. WCD Odisha releases Anganwadi Worker (12th pass) and Anganwadi Helper (10th/12th pass) vacancies on a rolling, district-wise basis throughout the year.

content_sections.heading: Eligibility Overview
content_sections.text: Nationality: Candidates must be Indian citizens as per the general eligibility conditions of Indian government recruitment. Residence: Many OSSC/OSSSC and district-level posts require an Odisha resident/nativity certificate, and passing Odia at the Middle English (Class 7) standard is required for several state posts; this requirement varies by notification. Educational Qualification: Ranges from 8th/10th pass for Group D posts to postgraduate and professional degrees for gazetted posts, as listed in the "Jobs by Qualification" section above. Age: See the Age Limit section below.

content_sections.heading: Age Limit
content_sections.text: The minimum age for most Odisha government posts is 18 years, and the general upper age limit ranges from 32 to 38 years for state posts, with age relaxation for SEBC, SC, ST, women and PwD candidates as per Odisha Government rules. The exact age limit and relaxation for a specific post is Not specified here and must be confirmed from that post's official notification, as it varies by recruiting body and post.

content_sections.heading: Salary
content_sections.text: Salaries for Odisha government jobs follow the state pay matrix. Group D posts start at the lower pay levels, while a Junior Clerk is placed in Level-4 with a basic pay of around ₹19,900 plus DA, HRA and other allowances; Group A officers draw considerably higher scales. The exact pay level and allowances for any post are specified in that post's official recruitment notification.

content_sections.heading: Selection Process
content_sections.text: Most Odisha government jobs involve a written examination, followed by a skill or physical test where applicable, and then document verification. OPSC posts may also include an interview stage, while OSSC, OSSSC and Odisha Police posts rely mainly on written and physical stages (PET/PST/PMT for uniformed posts). The exact selection stages vary by post and are described in the official notification.

content_sections.heading: Application Fee
content_sections.text: The application fee varies by recruiting body and post, and SC, ST and PwD candidates from Odisha are usually charged a reduced fee or exempted, subject to the rules in the specific notification. The exact fee and accepted payment mode for any post are Not specified here; refer to that post's official notification.

content_sections.heading: Required Documents
content_sections.text: Recent passport-size photograph and signature (as per notification format) Educational qualification certificates and mark sheets Date of birth proof (10th certificate/birth certificate) Caste/category certificate (SC/ST/SEBC), if applicable Odisha resident/nativity certificate, where required PwD certificate, if applicable Valid ID proof (Aadhaar/Voter ID/PAN) Experience certificate, if the post requires prior experience

content_sections.heading: How to Apply for Odisha Government Jobs Online 2026
content_sections.text: Open the official portal of the recruiting body (OPSC, OSSC, OSSSC or Odisha Police). Register with a valid email ID and mobile number to create your login. Fill the form with personal, educational and category details and upload your photo, signature and certificates. Pay the application fee online and submit before the last date. Save and print the confirmation page for future reference.

rows.purpose: OAS, gazetted and Group A/B recruitment

content_sections.heading: Frequently Asked Questions
content_sections.text: Salaries follow the state pay matrix. Group D posts start at lower levels, a Junior Clerk is placed in Level-4 with basic pay around ₹19,900 plus DA, HRA and other allowances, and Group A officers draw much higher scales. The exact pay band is stated in each official notification. Most involve a written exam, followed by a skill or physical test where applicable, and document verification. OPSC posts may add an interview; OSSC, OSSSC and Odisha Police rely mainly on written and physical stages. Focus on Odisha General Knowledge, history, geography and current affairs, along with reasoning, arithmetic and the post-specific syllabus. Solving previous year papers and taking mock tests improves speed and accuracy. The fee varies by recruiting body and post. SC, ST and PwD candidates are usually charged a reduced fee or exempted. The exact fee and payment mode are in each notification. Yes. Junior Clerk, Forest Guard, Nursing Officer and Odisha Police Constable posts, among others, are open to freshers with no prior work experience. OSSC mainly recruits Group C posts requiring graduation, such as Combined Graduate Level posts. OSSSC handles sub-ordinate/district-cadre Group C and Group D posts like Revenue Inspector, Nursing Officer and Forest Guard. Notifications are released throughout the year by OPSC, OSSC, OSSSC and various departments based on vacancy requirements. Checking this page daily helps you avoid missing a fresh update. Most regular OPSC, OSSC and OSSSC posts are permanent with pension and retirement benefits after a probation period. Any contractual or outsourced post is clearly stated in its notification. Always verify a vacancy on the official OPSC, OSSC, OSSSC or Odisha Police website before applying, and never pay agents who promise guaranteed jobs. Genuine recruitment charges only the official application fee. OSSSC Junior Clerk, Revenue Inspector and Nursing Officer posts, along with OPSC OAS and Odisha Police Constable, attract the highest number of applicants due to job security and steady promotion. Anganwadi Worker posts under WCD Odisha generally require 12th pass, while Anganwadi Helper posts generally require 10th or 12th pass; the exact qualification is stated in each district's notification. OSSC CGL (Combined Graduate Level) is a recruitment exam conducted by the Odisha Staff Selection Commission for Group C posts that require a graduate-level qualification. Candidates generally need their 10th/12th certificates, date of birth proof, category certificate (if applicable), Odisha nativity certificate where required, and a valid photo ID, as listed in the Required Documents section above. Several state government posts require passing Odia at the Middle English (Class 7) standard; this requirement is mentioned in the eligibility section of the relevant notification and does not apply uniformly to every post. Application status can generally be checked by logging into your registered account on the OSSC (ossc.gov.in) or OSS

faq.question: What is the salary for Odisha government jobs?
faq.answer: Salaries for Odisha government jobs follow the state pay matrix, with Group D posts starting at lower levels and a Junior Clerk placed in Level-4 with a basic pay around Rs.19,900, plus DA, HRA and other allowances. Group A officers draw much higher scales, and the exact pay band is stated in each official Odisha recruitment notification.

faq.question: What is the selection process for Odisha government jobs?
faq.answer: Most Odisha government jobs involve a written examination, followed by a skill or physical test where applicable, and then document verification. OPSC posts may also include an interview, while OSSC, OSSSC and Odisha Police posts rely mainly on written and physical stages.

faq.question: How can I prepare for OSSC and OSSSC exams?
faq.answer: Focus on Odisha General Knowledge, history, geography and current affairs of Odisha, along with reasoning, arithmetic and the post-specific syllabus. Solving previous year OSSC and OSSSC papers and taking regular mock tests improves your speed and accuracy for Odisha government exams.

faq.question: What is the application fee for Odisha government jobs?
faq.answer: The application fee varies by recruiting body and post, and SC, ST and PwD candidates of Odisha are usually charged a reduced fee or exempted. The exact fee and payment mode are mentioned in each OPSC, OSSC or OSSSC notification.

faq.question: Are there Odisha government jobs for freshers?
faq.answer: Yes, many Odisha government jobs such as Junior Clerk, Forest Guard, Nursing Officer and Odisha Police Constable are open to freshers with no prior work experience. Selection is based on age, qualification and exam performance rather than experience.

faq.question: What is the difference between OSSC and OSSSC in Odisha?
faq.answer: OSSC mainly recruits Group C posts that require graduation, such as the Combined Graduate Level posts, while OSSSC handles sub-ordinate and lower-qualification Group C and Group D posts like Revenue Inspector, Nursing Officer and Forest Guard. Both are major sources of Odisha government jobs every year.

faq.question: How often are new Odisha government jobs released?
faq.answer: New Odisha government job notifications are released throughout the year by OPSC, OSSC, OSSSC and various departments based on vacancy requirements. Checking this FreeJobAlert page daily ensures you never miss a fresh Odisha recruitment update.

faq.question: Are Odisha government jobs permanent?
faq.answer: Most regular OPSC, OSSC and OSSSC posts are permanent government positions with pension and retirement benefits, usually after a probation period. Any contractual or outsourced post is clearly mentioned in the official notification.

faq.question: How can I avoid fake Odisha government job notifications?
faq.answer: Always verify a vacancy on the official OPSC, OSSC, OSSSC or Odisha Police website before applying, and never pay money to agents who promise guaranteed jobs. Genuine Odisha government recruitment charges only the official application fee.

faq.question: Which Odisha government job is most popular among aspirants?
faq.answer: OSSSC Junior Clerk, Revenue Inspector and Nursing Officer posts, along with OPSC OAS and Odisha Police Constable, attract the highest number of applicants in Odisha every year due to job security and steady promotions.
